title: 10 precautions to take when using Ulmus rubra (Slippery Elm) for medicinal purposes
intro: When using Ulmus rubra, also known as slippery elm, for medicinal purposes, it is important to take several precautions. First, it is essential to consult with a healthcare provider before using slippery elm, especially for those who are pregnant or breastfeeding, as its safety during these times is not well-established. Additionally, individuals who have swallowing difficulties or who are experiencing a blocked intestine should avoid using slippery elm. Furthermore, slippery elm can interact with certain medications, such as antacids and blood thinners, so it is crucial to inform one's healthcare provider of any medications being taken. It is also important to note that long-term use of slippery elm may affect the absorption and effectiveness of some medications. Finally, while slippery elm is generally considered safe, it can cause side effects such as stomach discomfort and diarrhea in some individuals, so it is recommended to start with a low dose and increase gradually as tolerated.
